headline: Coinbase Reportedly Courts Anthropic to Bolster Exchange Security Infrastructure
summary: Coinbase reportedly pursues Anthropic's Mythos AI model access to bolster exchange security as Project Glasswing reshapes cyber defense.

headline: Software stocks are finally joining the tech rally
summary: The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF is set for its best two-day run since last April, led by heavyweights including Microsoft, Oracle, Palantir, Salesforce, and Palo Alto Networks.

headline: Palo Alto Networks University Deal Raises Geopolitical And Reputational Questions
summary: Palo Alto Networks and the University of Otago in New Zealand announced a cybersecurity education collaboration. University staff have raised concerns about the company’s reported ties to the Israeli government and military intelligence. The pushback highlights geopolitical questions around vendor selection in higher education cybersecurity programs. Palo Alto Networks, listed as NasdaqGS:PANW, is coming under fresh scrutiny that has little to do with product features or quarterly guidance...

headline: Palo Alto Networks Completes Acquisition of Koi to Secure the Agentic Endpoint
summary: Palo Alto Networks® (NASDAQ: PANW), the global cybersecurity leader, today announced the completion of its acquisition of Koi. With this acquisition, Palo Alto Networks defines a new category of protection: Agentic Endpoint Security (AES). Koi's unique solution to securing vibe coding agents and autonomous endpoint tools comes as the industry reaches an inflection point of agentic adoption.

headline: Palo Alto founder Nir Zuk enters deal to buy Liberty Bank – report
summary: Zuk has applied for approval to buy the biggest holding in Liberty from private-equity groups Stone Point and Reverence Capital.

headline: Jim Cramer Says Market Has It All Wrong on Cybersecurity and AI
summary: Jim Cramer made an argument on a recent Mad Money segment that deserves more attention: the market has been punishing cybersecurity stocks by lumping them in with traditional software, and that categorization is wrong. “AI isn’t gobbling them, it’s supporting them because it creates so many new vulnerabilities that hackers can exploit.” Every new AI ... Jim Cramer Says Market Has It All Wrong on Cybersecurity and AI
